bearable.definition: Something you can handle or live with, even if it is hard.
bearable.example: Talking to a friend made the sadness more bearable.

pain-free.example: We wish our children could have a pain-free life.

worry-free.example: A worry-free childhood is what every parent wants.

suffering.example: No parent wants their child suffering because of them.

conflict.definition: A serious disagreement or fight between people.
conflict.example: Conflict between parents can hurt children.

protect.definition: To keep someone safe from danger or harm.
protect.example: Parents want to protect their children from pain.

completely.example: It is impossible to protect them completely.

angst.definition: A deep feeling of worry or fear about life.
angst.example: Growing up comes with some angst.

unsolved.example: Life is full of unsolved mysteries.

mystery.definition: Something you do not understand or cannot explain.
mystery.example: Why he left remained a mystery.

longing.definition: A strong wish for something you do not have.
longing.example: She felt a deep longing for her old home.

loss.definition: The feeling of sadness when you lose something or someone.
loss.example: The loss of a pet can be very hard for a child.

alongside.example: Being alongside your child when they are sad helps a lot.

present.definition: Being there, giving your full attention.
present.example: Just being present for your child matters.

accepting.definition: Willing to receive or allow something without judging.
accepting.example: Being accepting of your child's feelings helps them heal.

fix.example: You may not be able to fix their pain.

denying.definition: Saying something is not true or not happening.
denying.example: Denying a child's feelings can make things worse.

attuned.definition: Aware of and sensitive to someone's feelings.
attuned.example: An attuned parent notices when their child is upset.

company.definition: Being with someone; having someone there with you.
company.example: Good company makes hard times easier.

unlucky.example: She was unlucky with her choice of partner.

choice.definition: The act of picking one thing over another.
choice.example: We cannot always control our choice of partner.

relationship.definition: The way two people are connected, especially as partners or family.
relationship.example: A good relationship with your child takes daily effort.
